remember: Students will be able to define what a stack is and explain its basic operations.
Students will be able to recall the basic properties of a stack, including last-in-first-out (LIFO) order and top element.
Students will be able to list the basic operations supported by a stack, including push, pop, and peek.
understand: Students will be able to explain the difference between a stack and a queue.
Students will be able to describe the real-world applications of stacks, such as parsing expressions and evaluating postfix notation.
Students will be able to understand the time and space complexities of basic stack operations.
apply: Students will be able to implement basic stack operations using an array or linked list.
Students will be able to use stacks to solve problems such as evaluating postfix expressions and parsing simple grammars.
Students will be able to analyze and optimize stack-based algorithms for efficiency.
analyze: Students will be able to compare and contrast different stack implementations and their trade-offs.
Students will be able to design and implement a stack-based data structure to solve a complex problem.
Students will be able to evaluate the time and space complexities of a stack-based algorithm and suggest improvements.

ncert_objectives: Explain the concept of a stack and its basic operations.
Describe the time and space complexities of basic stack operations.
Implement a stack using an array or linked list.
board_exam_objectives: Explain the basic properties of a stack, including LIFO order and top element (Class XII Computer Science CBSE Board Exam).
Describe the real-world applications of stacks and their significance in computer science (Class XII Computer Science CBSE Board Exam).
Implement a stack-based algorithm to solve a problem and analyze its time and space complexities (Class XII Computer Science CBSE Board Exam).
